text: I find myself enveloped by the coolness of glass and the sharp clarity of modern design. The emptiness and sterile orderliness evoke within me a sense of detachment and quiet anticipation. There is something both soothing and unsettling about such meticulously constructed spaces—they invite reflection, yet speak of transience and the impersonal. I am a passing witness, pausing amidst the hum of fluorescent lights and the silent potential of unused rooms.
